title: Stepped Hitlist and Select Cache

Stepped Hitlist and Select Cache

Stepped Hitlist and Select Cache

When retrieving data from a database, Uniface stores the retrieved records into an internal Uniface data structure called the stepped hitlist. This mechanism retrieves groups of records into the component, instead retrieving the complete table, which can cause the user to experience considerable delays.

When using the stepped hitlist, Uniface has two modes of operation—the select cache is either controlled or disabled. The implementation of this mechanism improves the performance in the following ways:

  • Connectors for record-level databases, which always retrieve complete rows, do not retrieve the same data twice from the DBMS.
  • In relational databases, the overhead caused by executing two queries, where one would be sufficient, is reduced.
  • The amount of data to be retrieved over a network is reduced.

This improvement is obtained by letting the connectors add complete records to the hitlist in the Select connector request when the select cache is controlled. With the select cache disabled, only the primary key fields or the physical record addresses of the selected records can be added to the hitlist in the Select connector requests. Uniface issues additional Fetch connector requests to fetch completed records when needed.

Uniface enables and disables the select cache dynamically, but fundamentally, Uniface will only cache rows that have no corresponding rows in an overflow table or are less than 512 bytes in size. (This is not an exact figure and it varies from version to version.)

Using the field uinfo->maxhits, the connector can indicate in the Info request whether it can support a stepped hitlist. To support the stepped hitlist it must be capable of fetching the result table in separate steps, while the fetches can be intermixed with other connector requests. Generally, relational databases that support cursors and record-level databases can support a stepped hitlist.

Related Links
  • uinfo

    Supplies Uniface with basic information about the DBMS and the connector.

  • Retrieval Processing

    Data is retrieved from the database when the user enters a retrieve profile (that is, a search string) in the desired fields and then enters ^RETRIEVE. Alternatively, ProcScript can be used to assign retrieve profiles to the desired fields in a component. When retrieving data from the database, Uniface selects the required data with the Select connector request, and fetches it into the component with the Fetch connector request.
